Daily DAP Accessibility Report — 2026-03-13#

Run ID: run-2026-03-13-5f6ae08f52 · Status: success · Source data: 2026-03-11 · Generated: 2026-03-13T09:30:37.423Z

About These Reports#

The Digital Analytics Program (DAP) is a U.S. government analytics service that collects website traffic data across participating federal agencies. DAP tracks page views, visitor counts, and usage patterns for hundreds of government websites, providing transparency into how the public engages with federal digital services.

This report measures the quality and accessibility of the top 100 most-visited U.S. government URLs as reported by DAP. Each day, Lighthouse scans are run against these URLs to measure:

  • Performance - How fast pages load for users (scores 0-100, higher is better)
  • Accessibility - How well pages work for users with disabilities, following WCAG guidelines (scores 0-100, higher is better)
  • Best Practices - Whether pages follow modern web development standards (scores 0-100, higher is better)
  • SEO - How well pages are optimized for search engines (scores 0-100, higher is better)

Accessibility findings come from axe-core, the industry-standard accessibility testing engine embedded in Lighthouse. The axe findings surface specific WCAG violations such as missing alternative text, insufficient color contrast, and missing form labels that make government websites harder to use for people with disabilities.

Traffic data reflects daily visitor counts from DAP. URLs are ranked by page load count, ensuring the most-used government pages are prioritized for quality measurement.

Accessibility Trend Narrative#

Over the past 15 days of data (2026-02-26 to 2026-03-12), government website accessibility scores have improved (+24.2 points, from 68 to 92.2). Performance scores have improved (+9.49 points, from 38 to 47.49).

Today's aggregate accessibility score of 91.91 reflects the mean Lighthouse accessibility score across 100 successfully scanned government URLs. A score above 90 indicates generally strong compliance with WCAG automated checks, though manual testing is always recommended to fully assess accessibility.

Common Accessibility Issues (Top 10)#

The following axe-core rules were most frequently violated across scanned URLs today. These patterns indicate systemic accessibility barriers present across multiple government websites.

Top axe-core accessibility rule violations across scanned URLs
Rule ID Description URLs affected Section 508 FPC
heading-orderHeading elements are not in a sequentially-descending order27WV, WH, LM
target-sizeTouch targets do not have sufficient size or spacing.27LM, LRS
landmark-one-mainDocument does not have a main landmark.23WV, WH, LM
color-contrastBackground and foreground colors do not have a sufficient contrast ratio.20LV, WPC
label-content-name-mismatchElements with visible text labels do not have matching accessible names.15WV, LV, WH, LM
link-nameLinks do not have a discernible name14WV, WH, LM
image-altImage elements do not have `[alt]` attributes11WV, WH
link-in-text-blockLinks rely on color to be distinguishable.9LV, WPC
listitemList items (`<li>`) are not contained within `<ul>`, `<ol>` or `<menu>` parent elements.7WV, WH, LM
html-has-lang`<html>` element does not have a `[lang]` attribute7WV, WH, LLCLA
Section 508 Functional Performance Criteria (FPC) key
WV
Without Vision
LV
Limited Vision
WPC
Without Perception of Color
WH
Without Hearing
LH
Limited Hearing
WS
Without Speech
LM
Limited Manipulation
LRS
Limited Reach and Strength
LLCLA
Limited Language, Cognitive, and Learning Abilities

FPC codes indicate which user groups are impacted by each accessibility barrier. See the Section 508 WCAG to FPC mapping and the EN 301 549 v3.2.1 Table B.2 for additional detail on how accessibility requirements map to functional needs.

Accessibility Findings

https://tools.usps.com

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 68

Axe findings: 10

`button`, `link`, and `menuitem` elements do not have accessible names. (rule: aria-command-name)

When an element doesn't have an accessible name, screen readers announce it with a generic name, making it unusable for users who rely on screen readers. Learn how to make command elements more accessible.

Section 508 FPC: WV, WH, LM

Affected elements (3):

Element 1

Element path: div.panel-heading > h4.panel-title > a#headingOneAnchor > span.down-arr

Snippet:

<span role="button" data-parent="#accordion" aria-expanded="true" aria-controls="collapseOne" class="down-arr">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element does not have text that is visible to screen readers
  • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  • Element has no title attribute

Element 2

Element path: div.panel-heading > h4.panel-title > a.collapsed > span.down-arr

Snippet:

<span role="button" data-parent="#accordion" aria-expanded="true" aria-controls="collapseOne" class="down-arr">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element does not have text that is visible to screen readers
  • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  • Element has no title attribute

Element 3

Element path: div.panel-heading > h4.panel-title > a.collapsed > span.down-arr

Snippet:

<span role="button" data-parent="#accordion" aria-expanded="true" aria-controls="collapseOne" class="down-arr">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element does not have text that is visible to screen readers
  • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  • Element has no title attribute
Elements with an ARIA `[role]` that require children to contain a specific `[role]` are missing some or all of those required children. (rule: aria-required-children)

Some ARIA parent roles must contain specific child roles to perform their intended accessibility functions. Learn more about roles and required children elements.

Section 508 FPC: WV, WH, LM

Label: Skip Quick Tools Links Quick Tools Track a Package Informed Delivery …

`[role]`s are not contained by their required parent element (rule: aria-required-parent)

Some ARIA child roles must be contained by specific parent roles to properly perform their intended accessibility functions. Learn more about ARIA roles and required parent element.

Section 508 FPC: WV, WH, LM

Heading elements are not in a sequentially-descending order (rule: heading-order)

Properly ordered headings that do not skip levels convey the semantic structure of the page, making it easier to navigate and understand when using assistive technologies. Learn more about heading order.

Links do not have a discernible name (rule: link-name)

Link text (and alternate text for images, when used as links) that is discernible, unique, and focusable improves the navigation experience for screen reader users. Learn how to make links accessible.

List items (`<li>`) are not contained within `<ul>`, `<ol>` or `<menu>` parent elements. (rule: listitem)

Screen readers require list items (`<li>`) to be contained within a parent `<ul>`, `<ol>` or `<menu>` to be announced properly. Learn more about proper list structure.

Document does not have a main landmark. (rule: landmark-one-main)

One main landmark helps screen reader users navigate a web page. Learn more about landmarks.

<html lang="en" class=" js flexbox flexboxlegacy canvas canvastext webgl touch geolocation postme…">

Download full axe findings JSON | Download full axe findings CSV

Accessibility Findings

https://forecast.weather.gov

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 61

Axe findings: 6

Background and foreground colors do not have a sufficient contrast ratio. (rule: color-contrast)

Low-contrast text is difficult or impossible for many users to read. Learn how to provide sufficient color contrast.

Section 508 FPC: LV, WPC

Affected elements (1):

Element 1

Element path: div.one-sixth-first > div#forecast-lookup > form#getForecast > input#inputstring

Snippet:

<input id="inputstring" name="inputstring" type="text" value="Enter location ..." onclick="this.value=''" autocomplete="off">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element has insufficient color contrast of 2.84 (foreground color: #999999, background color: #ffffff, font size: 8.0pt (10.6667px), font weight: normal). Expected contrast ratio of 4.5:1
`<html>` element does not have a `[lang]` attribute (rule: html-has-lang)

If a page doesn't specify a `lang` attribute, a screen reader assumes that the page is in the default language that the user chose when setting up the screen reader. If the page isn't actually in the default language, then the screen reader might not announce the page's text correctly. Learn more about the `lang` attribute.

Section 508 FPC: WV, WH, LLCLA

Affected elements (1):

Element 1

Element path: html

Snippet:

<html xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1999/xhtml">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • The <html> element does not have a lang attribute
Image elements do not have `[alt]` attributes (rule: image-alt)

Informative elements should aim for short, descriptive alternate text. Decorative elements can be ignored with an empty alt attribute. Learn more about the `alt` attribute.

Section 508 FPC: WV, WH

Affected elements (3):

Element 1

Element path: div.div-full > div#wwamap > div.USMap > img#us

Snippet:

<img id="us" src="https://www.weather.gov/wwamap/png/US.png" usemap="#US_large_imagemap_800_500">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element does not have an alt attribute
  • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  • Element has no title attribute
  • Element's default semantics were not overridden with role="none" or role="presentation"

Element 2

Element path: div.div-full > div#wwamap > div.USMap > img#alaska

Snippet:

<img id="alaska" src="https://www.weather.gov/wwamap/png/ak.png" usemap="#alaska_imagemap_130_115">

  • Element does not have an alt attribute
  • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  • Element has no title attribute
  • Element's default semantics were not overridden with role="none" or role="presentation"

Element 3

Element path: div.div-full > div#wwamap > div.USMap > img#hawaii

Snippet:

<img id="hawaii" src="https://www.weather.gov/wwamap/png/hi.png" usemap="#hawaii_imagemap">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element does not have an alt attribute
  • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  • Element has no title attribute
  • Element's default semantics were not overridden with role="none" or role="presentation"
Select elements do not have associated label elements. (rule: select-name)

Form elements without effective labels can create frustrating experiences for screen reader users. Learn more about the `select` element.

  • Element has no title attribute

`[aria-*]` attributes do not match their roles (rule: aria-allowed-attr)

Each ARIA `role` supports a specific subset of `aria-*` attributes. Mismatching these invalidates the `aria-*` attributes. Learn how to match ARIA attributes to their roles.

Section 508 FPC: WV, WH, LM

Affected elements (4):

Element 1

Element path: div#usps-carousel > div.carousel-inner > ol.carousel-indicators > li.active

Snippet:

<li data-target="#usps-carousel" data-slide-to="0" aria-checked="true" class="active">

Label: Slide 1

How to fix: Fix all of the following:

  • ARIA attribute is not allowed: aria-checked="true"

Element 2

Element path: div#usps-carousel > div.carousel-inner > ol.carousel-indicators > li

Snippet:

<li data-target="#usps-carousel" data-slide-to="1" aria-checked="false">

Label: Slide 2

How to fix: Fix all of the following:

  • ARIA attribute is not allowed: aria-checked="false"

Element 3

Element path: div#usps-carousel > div.carousel-inner > ol.carousel-indicators > li

Snippet:

<li data-target="#usps-carousel" data-slide-to="2" aria-checked="false">

Label: Slide 3

How to fix: Fix all of the following:

  • ARIA attribute is not allowed: aria-checked="false"

Element 4

Element path: div#usps-carousel > div.carousel-inner > ol.carousel-indicators > li

Snippet:

<li data-target="#usps-carousel" data-slide-to="3" aria-checked="false">

Label: Slide 4

How to fix: Fix all of the following:

  • ARIA attribute is not allowed: aria-checked="false"
Elements with an ARIA `[role]` that require children to contain a specific `[role]` are missing some or all of those required children. (rule: aria-required-children)

Some ARIA parent roles must contain specific child roles to perform their intended accessibility functions. Learn more about roles and required children elements.

Section 508 FPC: WV, WH, LM

Element path: div#g-navigation > div.nav-full > nav > ul.nav-list

Snippet:

<ul class="nav-list" role="menubar">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element has children which are not allowed: li[tabindex]
`[role]`s are not contained by their required parent element (rule: aria-required-parent)

Some ARIA child roles must be contained by specific parent roles to properly perform their intended accessibility functions. Learn more about ARIA roles and required parent element.

Section 508 FPC: WV, WH, LM

Affected elements (10):

Element path: nav > ul.nav-list > li.qt-nav > a.nav-first-element

Image elements do not have `[alt]` attributes (rule: image-alt)

Informative elements should aim for short, descriptive alternate text. Decorative elements can be ignored with an empty alt attribute. Learn more about the `alt` attribute.

Section 508 FPC: WV, WH

Affected elements (1):

Element 1

Element path: body > img

Snippet:

<img src="https://beacon.lynx.cognitivlabs.com/pixel/capture?tags=8dd8cf55-3ae9-49dc…">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element does not have an alt attribute
  • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  • Element has no title attribute
  • Element's default semantics were not overridden with role="none" or role="presentation"
Links do not have a discernible name (rule: link-name)

Link text (and alternate text for images, when used as links) that is discernible, unique, and focusable improves the navigation experience for screen reader users. Learn how to make links accessible.

Section 508 FPC: WV, WH, LM

  • Element has no title attribute

List items (`<li>`) are not contained within `<ul>`, `<ol>` or `<menu>` parent elements. (rule: listitem)

Screen readers require list items (`<li>`) to be contained within a parent `<ul>`, `<ol>` or `<menu>` to be announced properly. Learn more about proper list structure.

`[user-scalable="no"]` is used in the `<meta name="viewport">` element or the `[maximum-scale]` attribute is less than 5. (rule: meta-viewport)

Disabling zooming is problematic for users with low vision who rely on screen magnification to properly see the contents of a web page. Learn more about the viewport meta tag.

Section 508 FPC: LV

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • user-scalable=no on <meta> tag disables zooming on mobile devices

Low-contrast text is difficult or impossible for many users to read. Learn how to provide sufficient color contrast.

Heading elements are not in a sequentially-descending order (rule: heading-order)

Properly ordered headings that do not skip levels convey the semantic structure of the page, making it easier to navigate and understand when using assistive technologies. Learn more about heading order.

  • Heading order invalid
`<html>` element does not have a `[lang]` attribute (rule: html-has-lang)

If a page doesn't specify a `lang` attribute, a screen reader assumes that the page is in the default language that the user chose when setting up the screen reader. If the page isn't actually in the default language, then the screen reader might not announce the page's text correctly. Learn more about the `lang` attribute.

Section 508 FPC: WV, WH, LLCLA

Affected elements (1):

Element 1

Element path: html

Snippet:

<html>

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • The <html> element does not have a lang attribute
Links do not have a discernible name (rule: link-name)

Link text (and alternate text for images, when used as links) that is discernible, unique, and focusable improves the navigation experience for screen reader users. Learn how to make links accessible.

Section 508 FPC: WV, WH, LM

  • Fix any of the following:

  • Element has no title attribute

  • Element has no title attribute
List items (`<li>`) are not contained within `<ul>`, `<ol>` or `<menu>` parent elements. (rule: listitem)

Screen readers require list items (`<li>`) to be contained within a parent `<ul>`, `<ol>` or `<menu>` to be announced properly. Learn more about proper list structure.

Document does not have a main landmark. (rule: landmark-one-main)

One main landmark helps screen reader users navigate a web page. Learn more about landmarks.

Section 508 FPC: WV, WH, LM

Affected elements (1):

Element 1

Element path: html

Snippet:

<html xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1999/xhtml">

https://ssa.gov

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 90

Axe findings: 4

Background and foreground colors do not have a sufficient contrast ratio. (rule: color-contrast)

Low-contrast text is difficult or impossible for many users to read. Learn how to provide sufficient color contrast.

Section 508 FPC: LV, WPC

Affected elements (4):

Element 1

Element path: ul.cta-button-list__list > li.cta-button > a.cta-button__link > span.cta-button__label

Snippet:

<span class="cta-button__label">

Label: Get a benefits estimate

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element has insufficient color contrast of 4.35 (foreground color: #0076d6, background color: #f7f9fa, font size: 13.5pt (18px), font weight: bold). Expected contrast ratio of 4.5:1

Element path: ul.cta-button-list__list > li.cta-button > a.cta-button__link > span.cta-button__label

Heading elements are not in a sequentially-descending order (rule: heading-order)

Properly ordered headings that do not skip levels convey the semantic structure of the page, making it easier to navigate and understand when using assistive technologies. Learn more about heading order.

Section 508 FPC: WV, WH, LM

Affected elements (3):

Element 1

Element path: li.link-collection-card > div.link-collection-card__container > div.link-collection-card__header > h4.link-collection-card__heading

Snippet:

<h4 class="link-collection-card__heading usa-card__heading">

Label: Age milestones

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Heading order invalid

Element 2

Element path: li.link-collection-card > div.link-collection-card__container > div.link-collection-card__header > h4.link-collection-card__heading

Snippet:

<h4 class="link-collection-card__heading usa-card__heading">

Label: Documents

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Heading order invalid

Element 3

Element path: li.service-card > div.service-card__container > div.service-card__header > h4.service-card__heading

Snippet:

<h4 class="service-card__heading usa-card__heading">

Label: Businesses and employers

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Heading order invalid
Image elements do not have `[alt]` attributes (rule: image-alt)

Informative elements should aim for short, descriptive alternate text. Decorative elements can be ignored with an empty alt attribute. Learn more about the `alt` attribute.

Section 508 FPC: WV, WH

Affected elements (1):

Element 1

Element path: div > div.blazy > div.media > img.media__element

Snippet:

<img decoding="async" class="media__element b-lazy" loading="lazy" src="" width="480" height="360">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element does not have an alt attribute
  • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  • Element has no title attribute
  • Element's default semantics were not overridden with role="none" or role="presentation"
Elements with visible text labels do not have matching accessible names. (rule: label-content-name-mismatch)

Visible text labels that do not match the accessible name can result in a confusing experience for screen reader users. Learn more about accessible names.

Section 508 FPC: WV, LV, WH, LM

Affected elements (6):

Element 1

Element path: div.usa-nav-container > div.region > div#logo > a.usa-logo__text

<a class="usa-logo__text ssa-logo__text" href="/" accesskey="1" aria-label="Home">

  • Text inside the element is not included in the accessible name

Accessibility Findings

https://justice.gov

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 97

Axe findings: 1

Lists do not contain only `<li>` elements and script supporting elements (`<script>` and `<template>`). (rule: list)

Screen readers have a specific way of announcing lists. Ensuring proper list structure aids screen reader output. Learn more about proper list structure.

Section 508 FPC: WV, WH

Affected elements (1):

Element 1

Element path: div > div.most-wanted-view > div.item-list > ul.slick-initialized

Snippet:

<ul class="slick-initialized slick-slider slick-dotted">

Label: Oscar Noe Medina Gonzalez DEA Field Office: New York Southern Wanted For: : CON…

How to fix: Fix all of the following:

  • List element has direct children that are not allowed: div

Download full axe findings JSON | Download full axe findings CSV

Accessibility Findings

https://travel.state.gov

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 93

Axe findings: 2

`[user-scalable="no"]` is used in the `<meta name="viewport">` element or the `[maximum-scale]` attribute is less than 5. (rule: meta-viewport)

Disabling zooming is problematic for users with low vision who rely on screen magnification to properly see the contents of a web page. Learn more about the viewport meta tag.

Section 508 FPC: LV

Affected elements (1):

Element 1

Element path: head > meta

Snippet:

<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0, maximum-scale=1.0, user-scalable=no">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • user-scalable=no on <meta> tag disables zooming on mobile devices
Document does not have a main landmark. (rule: landmark-one-main)

One main landmark helps screen reader users navigate a web page. Learn more about landmarks.

Section 508 FPC: WV, WH, LM

Affected elements (1):

Element 1

Element path: html

Snippet:

<html lang="en">

How to fix: Fix all of the following:

  • Document does not have a main landmark

Download full axe findings JSON | Download full axe findings CSV

Accessibility Findings

https://cdc.gov

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 91

Axe findings: 3

Links do not have a discernible name (rule: link-name)

Link text (and alternate text for images, when used as links) that is discernible, unique, and focusable improves the navigation experience for screen reader users. Learn how to make links accessible.

Section 508 FPC: WV, WH, LM

Affected elements (1):

Element 1

Element path: nav.mobile-navbar > div.mobile-navbar_top > div.d-flex > a#cdc-mobile-search-toggle

Snippet:

<a id="cdc-mobile-search-toggle" class="nav-link" href="#" aria-controls="cdc-mobile-search-form">

How to fix: Fix all of the following:

  • Element is in tab order and does not have accessible text
  • Fix any of the following:
  • Element does not have text that is visible to screen readers
  • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  • Element has no title attribute
Skip links are not focusable. (rule: skip-link)

Including a skip link can help users skip to the main content to save time. Learn more about skip links.

Section 508 FPC: WV, WH, LM

Affected elements (1):

Element 1

Element path: body.cdc-post-type--cdc-homepage > div#skipmenu > a.skippy

Snippet:

<a class="skippy visually-hidden-focusable" href="#headerSearch" tabindex="2">

Label: Skip to search

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • No skip link target
Some elements have a `[tabindex]` value greater than 0 (rule: tabindex)

A value greater than 0 implies an explicit navigation ordering. Although technically valid, this often creates frustrating experiences for users who rely on assistive technologies. Learn more about the `tabindex` attribute.

Section 508 FPC: WV, WH, LM

Affected elements (3):

Element 1

Element path: body.cdc-post-type--cdc-homepage > div#skipmenu > a.skippy

Snippet:

<a class="skippy visually-hidden-focusable" href="#content" tabindex="1">

Label: Skip to site content

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element has a tabindex greater than 0

Element 2

Element path: body.cdc-post-type--cdc-homepage > div#skipmenu > a.skippy

Snippet:

<a class="skippy visually-hidden-focusable" href="#headerSearch" tabindex="2">

Label: Skip to search

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element has a tabindex greater than 0

Element 3

Element path: div#hero-carousel > div.carousel-indicators > div.carousel-indicators-wrapper > button#animation-toggle

Snippet:

<button id="animation-toggle" aria-pressed="true" tabindex="3">

Label: Pause carousel

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element has a tabindex greater than 0

Accessibility Findings

https://studentaid.gov

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 87

Axe findings: 4

Background and foreground colors do not have a sufficient contrast ratio. (rule: color-contrast)

Low-contrast text is difficult or impossible for many users to read. Learn how to provide sufficient color contrast.

Section 508 FPC: LV, WPC

Affected elements (5):

Element 1

Element path: div.fsa-md-padding-left > div.d-flex > div.d-flex > div.font-weight-bold

Snippet:

<div _ngcontent-opw-c151="" class="font-weight-bold text-center mr-xl-2 mb-2 mb-xl-0 fsa-color-green-medium f…">

Label: 01

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element has insufficient color contrast of 3.32 (foreground color: #3ea11b, background color: #ffffff, font size: 13.5pt (18px), font weight: bold). Expected contrast ratio of 4.5:1

Element 2

Element path: div.fsa-md-padding-left > div.d-flex > div.d-flex > div.font-weight-bold

<div _ngcontent-opw-c151="" class="font-weight-bold text-center mr-xl-2 mb-2 mb-xl-0 fsa-color-green-medium f…">

Heading elements are not in a sequentially-descending order (rule: heading-order)

Properly ordered headings that do not skip levels convey the semantic structure of the page, making it easier to navigate and understand when using assistive technologies. Learn more about heading order.

Section 508 FPC: WV, WH, LM

Affected elements (1):

Element 1

Element path: div.home-banner > div.row > div.col-md-5 > h4#fsa_Title_PopularTasks

Snippet:

<h4 _ngcontent-opw-c153="" id="fsa_Title_PopularTasks" class="text-left fsa-mb-px-16 fsa-color-white">

Label: POPULAR TOPICS

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Heading order invalid
Links rely on color to be distinguishable. (rule: link-in-text-block)

Low-contrast text is difficult or impossible for many users to read. Link text that is discernible improves the experience for users with low vision. Learn how to make links distinguishable.

Section 508 FPC: LV, WPC

Affected elements (2):

Element 1

Element path: div.fsa-banner-content-body > span > div > a

Snippet:

<a href="/announcements-events/idr-court-actions">

Label: visit StudentAid.gov/courtactions

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • The link has insufficient color contrast of 1.64:1 with the surrounding text. (Minimum contrast is 3:1, link text: #0000ee, surrounding text: #212529)
  • The link has no styling (such as underline) to distinguish it from the surrounding text

Element 2

Element path: div.fsa-banner-content-body > span > div > a

Snippet:

<a href="/fsa-id/sign-in/landing/?redirectTo=/my-aid">

Label: Log in

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • The link has insufficient color contrast of 1.64:1 with the surrounding text. (Minimum contrast is 3:1, link text: #0000ee, surrounding text: #212529)
  • The link has no styling (such as underline) to distinguish it from the surrounding text
Touch targets do not have sufficient size or spacing. (rule: target-size)

Touch targets with sufficient size and spacing help users who may have difficulty targeting small controls to activate the targets. Learn more about touch targets.

Section 508 FPC: LM, LRS

Affected elements (1):

Element 1

Element path: footer#fsa-universal-footer > section.fsa-top-links > a#fsa_Link_FooterLevel1SiteSurvey

Snippet:

<a class="fsa-footer-link fsa-delimited-link-mobile fsa-top-footer-links bold" id="fsa_Link_FooterLevel1SiteSurvey" href="javascript:window.KAMPYLE_ONSITE_SDK.showForm('308');">

Label: Site Survey

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Target has insufficient size (61px by 23px, should be at least 24px by 24px)
  • Target has insufficient space to its closest neighbors. Safe clickable space has a diameter of 23px instead of at least 24px.

Accessibility Findings

https://radar.weather.gov

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 77

Axe findings: 2

`<html>` element does not have a `[lang]` attribute (rule: html-has-lang)

If a page doesn't specify a `lang` attribute, a screen reader assumes that the page is in the default language that the user chose when setting up the screen reader. If the page isn't actually in the default language, then the screen reader might not announce the page's text correctly. Learn more about the `lang` attribute.

Section 508 FPC: WV, WH, LLCLA

Affected elements (1):

Element 1

Element path: html

Snippet:

<html>

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • The <html> element does not have a lang attribute
`[user-scalable="no"]` is used in the `<meta name="viewport">` element or the `[maximum-scale]` attribute is less than 5. (rule: meta-viewport)

Disabling zooming is problematic for users with low vision who rely on screen magnification to properly see the contents of a web page. Learn more about the viewport meta tag.

Section 508 FPC: LV

Affected elements (1):

Element 1

Element path: head > meta

Snippet:

<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1, maximum-scale=1, user-scalable=0">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • user-scalable on <meta> tag disables zooming on mobile devices

Download full axe findings JSON | Download full axe findings CSV

Document does not have a main landmark. (rule: landmark-one-main)

One main landmark helps screen reader users navigate a web page. Learn more about landmarks.

  • Target has insufficient space to its closest neighbors. Safe clickable space has a diameter of 16.4px instead of at least 24px.

Accessibility Findings

https://egov.uscis.gov

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 90

Axe findings: 1

The document uses `<meta http-equiv="refresh">` (rule: meta-refresh)

Users do not expect a page to refresh automatically, and doing so will move focus back to the top of the page. This may create a frustrating or confusing experience. Learn more about the refresh meta tag.

Section 508 FPC: WV, WH, LM

Affected elements (1):

Element 1

Element path: head > meta

Snippet:

<meta http-equiv="refresh" content="360">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • <meta> tag forces timed refresh of page (less than 20 hours)

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 98

Axe findings: 1

Heading elements are not in a sequentially-descending order (rule: heading-order)

Properly ordered headings that do not skip levels convey the semantic structure of the page, making it easier to navigate and understand when using assistive technologies. Learn more about heading order.

Section 508 FPC: WV, WH, LM

https://fda.gov

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 95

Axe findings: 2

Heading elements are not in a sequentially-descending order (rule: heading-order)

Properly ordered headings that do not skip levels convey the semantic structure of the page, making it easier to navigate and understand when using assistive technologies. Learn more about heading order.

Section 508 FPC: WV, WH, LM

Affected elements (1):

Element 1

Element path: div.fda-ft-container > div.fda-ft-heading > div.fda-ft-heading-text > h3.fda-ft-heading-title

Label: Featured Topics

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Heading order invalid
Some elements have a `[tabindex]` value greater than 0 (rule: tabindex)

A value greater than 0 implies an explicit navigation ordering. Although technically valid, this often creates frustrating experiences for users who rely on assistive technologies. Learn more about the `tabindex` attribute.

Section 508 FPC: WV, WH, LM

Affected elements (3):

Element 1

Element path: div#quicklinks > ul.list-inline > li > a.btn

Snippet:

<a href="#main-content" class="btn btn-default margin-top-3" tabindex="1" data-once="download-role-button lang-fix-multilingual">

Label: Skip to main content

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element has a tabindex greater than 0

Element 2

Element path: div#quicklinks > ul.list-inline > li > a.btn

Snippet:

<a href="#search-form" class="btn btn-default margin-top-3" tabindex="1" data-once="download-role-button lang-fix-multilingual">

Label: Skip to FDA Search

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element has a tabindex greater than 0

Element 3

Element path: div#quicklinks > ul.list-inline > li > a.btn

Snippet:

<a href="#footer" class="btn btn-default margin-top-3" tabindex="1" data-once="download-role-button lang-fix-multilingual">

Label: Skip to footer links

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element has a tabindex greater than 0

Accessibility Findings

https://science.nasa.gov

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 95

Axe findings: 1

Buttons do not have an accessible name (rule: button-name)

When a button doesn't have an accessible name, screen readers announce it as "button", making it unusable for users who rely on screen readers. Learn how to make buttons more accessible.

Section 508 FPC: WV, WH

Affected elements (4):

Element 1

Element path: header.hds-nasa-header > div.usa-nav-container > div.grid-col > button#global-navigation-trigger--mobile

Snippet:

<button id="global-navigation-trigger--mobile" class="hds-global-menu-toggle menu-toggle menu-mobile-toggle" aria-expanded="false" data-menu-action="open" data-menu-location="left">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element does not have inner text that is visible to screen readers
  • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  • Element has no title attribute
  • Element does not have an implicit (wrapped) <label>
  • Element does not have an explicit <label>
  • Element's default semantics were not overridden with role="none" or role="presentation"

Element 2

Element path: header.hds-nasa-header > div.usa-nav-container > div.grid-col > button.hds-search-panel-mobile-trigger

  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  • Element has no title attribute
  • Element does not have an implicit (wrapped) <label>
  • Element does not have an explicit <label>
  • Element's default semantics were not overridden with role="none" or role="presentation"

  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  • Element has no title attribute
  • Element does not have an implicit (wrapped) <label>
  • Element does not have an explicit <label>
  • Element's default semantics were not overridden with role="none" or role="presentation"

Links rely on color to be distinguishable. (rule: link-in-text-block)

Low-contrast text is difficult or impossible for many users to read. Link text that is discernible improves the experience for users with low vision. Learn how to make links distinguishable.

Section 508 FPC: LV, WPC

Affected elements (1):

Element 1

Element path: div.usa-grid > div.usa-footer-logo > p > a

Snippet:

<a href="https://www.tsp.gov/contact/">

Label: contact us

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • The link has insufficient color contrast of 1.5:1 with the surrounding text. (Minimum contrast is 3:1, link text: #d6d7d9, surrounding text: #aeb0b5)
  • The link has no styling (such as underline) to distinguish it from the surrounding text

`<html>` element does not have a `[lang]` attribute (rule: html-has-lang)

If a page doesn't specify a `lang` attribute, a screen reader assumes that the page is in the default language that the user chose when setting up the screen reader. If the page isn't actually in the default language, then the screen reader might not announce the page's text correctly. Learn more about the `lang` attribute.

Section 508 FPC: WV, WH, LLCLA

Affected elements (1):

Element 1

Element path: html

Snippet:

<html>

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • The <html> element does not have a lang attribute

If a page doesn't specify a `lang` attribute, a screen reader assumes that the page is in the default language that the user chose when setting up the screen reader. Links do not have a discernible name (rule: link-name)

Link text (and alternate text for images, when used as links) that is discernible, unique, and focusable improves the navigation experience for screen reader users. Learn how to make links accessible.

Section 508 FPC: WV, WH, LM

Touch targets do not have sufficient size or spacing. (rule: target-size)

Touch targets with sufficient size and spacing help users who may have difficulty targeting small controls to activate the targets. Learn more about touch targets.

Section 508 FPC: LM, LRS

Affected elements (52):

Element 1

Element path: div.SPCMain2 > div#midBlock > div#searchBox > a.SPC_twitter

Snippet:

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Target has insufficient size (20px by 20px, should be at least 24px by 24px)
  • Target has insufficient space to its closest neighbors. Safe clickable space has a diameter of 20px instead of at least 24px.

  • Target has insufficient size (45.3px by 12px, should be at least 24px by 24px)
  • Target has insufficient space to its closest neighbors. Safe clickable space has a diameter of 14.4px instead of at least 24px.

Snippet:

<a href="//www.weather.gov/careers">

Document does not have a main landmark. (rule: landmark-one-main)

One main landmark helps screen reader users navigate a web page. Learn more about landmarks.

Section 508 FPC: WV, WH, LM

Affected elements (1):

Element 1

Element path: html

Snippet:

<html xmlns="//www.w3.org/1999/xhtml">

How to fix: Fix all of the following:

  • Document does not have a main landmark
`<td>` elements in a large `<table>` do not have table headers. (rule: td-has-header)

Screen readers have features to make navigating tables easier. Ensuring that `<td>` elements in a large table (3 or more cells in width and height) have an associated table header may improve the experience for screen reader users. Learn more about table headers.

Section 508 FPC: WV, WH

Affected elements (1):

Element 1

Element path: tbody > tr > td > table

Snippet:

<table cellspacing="1" cellpadding="2" border="0" width="100%">

Label: Hazard Fri (03/13) Sat (03/14) Sun (03/15) Mon (03/16) Tue (03/17) Wed (03/18) …

How to fix: Fix all of the following:

  • Some non-empty data cells do not have table headers

Download full axe findings JSON | Download full axe findings CSV

Accessibility Findings

https://ecfr.gov

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 73

Axe findings: 7

`[aria-*]` attributes do not match their roles (rule: aria-allowed-attr)

Each ARIA `role` supports a specific subset of `aria-*` attributes. Mismatching these invalidates the `aria-*` attributes. Learn how to match ARIA attributes to their roles.

Section 508 FPC: WV, WH, LM

Affected elements (1):

Element 1

Element path: div.row > div.col-xs-12 > div > div#suggestions

Snippet:

<div id="suggestions" data-controller="suggestions autocomplete" data-autocomplete-url-value="/suggestions" data-autocomplete-delay-value="300" aria-expanded="false" data-autocomplete-ready-value="true">

Label:

How to fix: Fix all of the following:

  • ARIA attribute is not allowed: aria-expanded="false"
Buttons do not have an accessible name (rule: button-name)

When a button doesn't have an accessible name, screen readers announce it as "button", making it unusable for users who rely on screen readers. Learn how to make buttons more accessible.

Section 508 FPC: WV, WH

Affected elements (1):

Element 1

Element path: form.simple_form > div.input-group > span.input-group-btn > button.btn

Snippet:

<button class="btn btn-optional" type="button" data-suggestions-target="nonModalSearchIcon" data-action="click-&gt;placeholder#suggestions">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element does not have inner text that is visible to screen readers
  • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  • Element has no title attribute
  • Element does not have an implicit (wrapped) <label>
  • Element does not have an explicit <label>
  • Element's default semantics were not overridden with role="none" or role="presentation"
Background and foreground colors do not have a sufficient contrast ratio. (rule: color-contrast)

Low-contrast text is difficult or impossible for many users to read. Learn how to provide sufficient color contrast.

Section 508 FPC: LV, WPC

Label: Sign In / Sign Up

Heading elements are not in a sequentially-descending order (rule: heading-order)

Properly ordered headings that do not skip levels convey the semantic structure of the page, making it easier to navigate and understand when using assistive technologies. Learn more about heading order.

Section 508 FPC: WV, WH, LM

Element path: div.box > div.seal-block > div.seal-content > h6

Links rely on color to be distinguishable. (rule: link-in-text-block)

Low-contrast text is difficult or impossible for many users to read. Link text that is discernible improves the experience for users with low vision. Learn how to make links distinguishable.

Section 508 FPC: LV, WPC

Affected elements (1):

Element 1

Element path: div.row > div.page-summary > p > a

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • The link has no styling (such as underline) to distinguish it from the surrounding text
Touch targets do not have sufficient size or spacing. (rule: target-size)

Touch targets with sufficient size and spacing help users who may have difficulty targeting small controls to activate the targets. Learn more about touch targets.

Links do not have a discernible name (rule: link-name)

Link text (and alternate text for images, when used as links) that is discernible, unique, and focusable improves the navigation experience for screen reader users. Learn how to make links accessible.

Section 508 FPC: WV, WH, LM

Element 1

Element path: div.grid-row > div.grid-col-12 > div.hds-image-download-wrapper > a.hds-image-download-link

  • Element has no title attribute

https://fas.usda.gov

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 97

Axe findings: 2

`[aria-hidden="true"]` elements contain focusable descendents (rule: aria-hidden-focus)

Focusable descendents within an `[aria-hidden="true"]` element prevent those interactive elements from being available to users of assistive technologies like screen readers. Learn how `aria-hidden` affects focusable elements.

Visible text labels that do not match the accessible name can result in a confusing experience for screen reader users. Learn more about accessible names.

Element path: nav.usa-nav > div.region > div.footer > div#search-block-wrap-1

  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty

Background and foreground colors do not have a sufficient contrast ratio. (rule: color-contrast)

Low-contrast text is difficult or impossible for many users to read. Learn how to provide sufficient color contrast.

Accessibility Findings

https://esta.cbp.dhs.gov

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 85

Axe findings: 4

Image elements do not have `[alt]` attributes (rule: image-alt)

Informative elements should aim for short, descriptive alternate text. Decorative elements can be ignored with an empty alt attribute. Learn more about the `alt` attribute.

Section 508 FPC: WV, WH

Affected elements (1):

Element 1

Element path: div.toggle-language > span.dropdown > button#languageDrop > img

Snippet:

<img _ngcontent-ng-c799267185="" src="assets/images/flags/usUkFlag.png">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element does not have an alt attribute
  • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  • Element has no title attribute
  • Element's default semantics were not overridden with role="none" or role="presentation"
Links rely on color to be distinguishable. (rule: link-in-text-block)

Low-contrast text is difficult or impossible for many users to read. Link text that is discernible improves the experience for users with low vision. Learn how to make links distinguishable.

Section 508 FPC: LV, WPC

Affected elements (1):

Element 1

Element path: div.col-xs-12 > ul > li#welcome.whoYouAre.1 > a.strong

Snippet:

<a tabindex="0" data-target="#visaModal" data-toggle="modal" class="strong esta-link" href="#">

Label: Visa Waiver Program

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • The link has insufficient color contrast of 1.18:1 with the surrounding text. (Minimum contrast is 3:1, link text: #00416a, surrounding text: #333333)
  • The link has no styling (such as underline) to distinguish it from the surrounding text
Lists do not contain only `<li>` elements and script supporting elements (`<script>` and `<template>`). (rule: list)

Screen readers have a specific way of announcing lists. Ensuring proper list structure aids screen reader output. Learn more about proper list structure.

Section 508 FPC: WV, WH

Affected elements (1):

Element 1

Element path: div.container > div.row > div.col-xs-12 > ul

Snippet:

<ul _ngcontent-ng-c2940030545="">

Label: About ESTA and The Visa Waiver Program What is the difference between an ESTA …

How to fix: Fix all of the following:

  • List element has direct children that are not allowed: div
List items (`<li>`) are not contained within `<ul>`, `<ol>` or `<menu>` parent elements. (rule: listitem)

Screen readers require list items (`<li>`) to be contained within a parent `<ul>`, `<ol>` or `<menu>` to be announced properly. Learn more about proper list structure.

Section 508 FPC: WV, WH, LM

https://my.uscis.gov

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 89

Axe findings: 2

`[aria-*]` attributes do not match their roles (rule: aria-allowed-attr)

Each ARIA `role` supports a specific subset of `aria-*` attributes. Mismatching these invalidates the `aria-*` attributes. Learn how to match ARIA attributes to their roles.

Section 508 FPC: WV, WH, LM

Affected elements (1):

Element 1

Element path: div.uscis-row > div.col-span-6 > div.mobile-header > a#toggle-sections

Label: Menu

How to fix: Fix all of the following:

  • ARIA attribute is not allowed: aria-expanded="false"
`[user-scalable="no"]` is used in the `<meta name="viewport">` element or the `[maximum-scale]` attribute is less than 5. (rule: meta-viewport)

Disabling zooming is problematic for users with low vision who rely on screen magnification to properly see the contents of a web page. Learn more about the viewport meta tag.

Section 508 FPC: LV

Affected elements (1):

Element 1

Element path: head > meta

Snippet:

<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width,height=device-height,user-scalable=no,initial-scale=1.0…">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • user-scalable=no on <meta> tag disables zooming on mobile devices

https://noaa.gov

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 96

Axe findings: 1

Touch targets do not have sufficient size or spacing. (rule: target-size)

Touch targets with sufficient size and spacing help users who may have difficulty targeting small controls to activate the targets. Learn more about touch targets.

Section 508 FPC: LM, LRS

Affected elements (2):

Element 1

Element path: div#footer-menus > ul#menu--footer-2 > li.menu__item > a.menu__link

Label: Protecting Your Privacy

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Target has insufficient size (158.8px by 22px, should be at least 24px by 24px)
  • Target has insufficient space to its closest neighbors. Safe clickable space has a diameter of 22px instead of at least 24px.

Element 2

Element path: div#footer-menus > ul#menu--footer-2 > li.menu__item > a.menu__link

Snippet:

<a href="https://vote.gov/" class="menu__link" data-ga="Footer:Vote.gov">

Label: Vote.gov

  • Target has insufficient space to its closest neighbors. Safe clickable space has a diameter of 22px instead of at least 24px.

Accessibility Findings

https://ncei.noaa.gov

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 98

Axe findings: 2

Document does not have a main landmark. (rule: landmark-one-main)

One main landmark helps screen reader users navigate a web page. Learn more about landmarks.

Section 508 FPC: WV, WH, LM

Affected elements (1):

Element 1

Element path: html.js

Snippet:

<html lang="en" dir="ltr" prefix="content: http://purl.org/rss/1.0/modules/content/  dc: http://purl.org/dc/…" class="js">

Elements with visible text labels do not have matching accessible names. (rule: label-content-name-mismatch)

Visible text labels that do not match the accessible name can result in a confusing experience for screen reader users. Learn more about accessible names.

Section 508 FPC: WV, LV, WH, LM

Affected elements (1):

Element 1

Element path: header > nav.navbar > div.container > button.navbar-toggler

Snippet:

<button class="navbar-toggler" type="button" data-toggle="collapse" data-target=".navbar-collapse" aria-controls="navbar-collapse" aria-expanded="false" aria-label="Toggle navigation">

Label: MENU

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Text inside the element is not included in the accessible name

https://home.treasury.gov

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 97

Axe findings: 1

Touch targets do not have sufficient size or spacing. (rule: target-size)

Touch targets with sufficient size and spacing help users who may have difficulty targeting small controls to activate the targets. Learn more about touch targets.

Section 508 FPC: LM, LRS

Affected elements (2):

Element 1

Element path: div.site-menu > ul > li > a

Snippet:

<a href="https://home.treasury.gov/subfooter/google-privacy-policy">

Label: Google Privacy

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Target has insufficient size (85.3px by 18px, should be at least 24px by 24px)
  • Target has insufficient space to its closest neighbors. Safe clickable space has a diameter of 20.6px instead of at least 24px.

Element 2

Element path: div.site-menu > ul > li > a

Snippet:

<a href="/about/careers-at-treasury" data-display-mobile="0" data-display-desktop="0" data-drupal-link-system-path="node/4341">

Label: Careers

  • Target has insufficient size (43.6px by 18px, should be at least 24px by 24px)
  • Target has insufficient space to its closest neighbors. Safe clickable space has a diameter of 20.6px instead of at least 24px.

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 97

Axe findings: 2

Elements use prohibited ARIA attributes (rule: aria-prohibited-attr)

Using ARIA attributes in roles where they are prohibited can mean that important information is not communicated to users of assistive technologies. Learn more about prohibited ARIA roles.

Affected elements (1):

Element 1

Element path: div.paragraph__column > div.px-video-container > div#progress-bar > div#progress

Snippet:

<div class="progress" id="progress" aria-label="Video: " style="width: 0%;">

Label: Video:

How to fix: Fix all of the following:

  • aria-label attribute cannot be used on a div with no valid role attribute.

https://store.usps.com

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 70

Axe findings: 9

Elements with an ARIA `[role]` that require children to contain a specific `[role]` are missing some or all of those required children. (rule: aria-required-children)

Some ARIA parent roles must contain specific child roles to perform their intended accessibility functions. Learn more about roles and required children elements.

Section 508 FPC: WV, WH, LM

Affected elements (1):

Element 1

Element path: div#g-navigation > div.nav-full > nav > ul.nav-list

Snippet:

<ul class="nav-list" role="menubar">

Label: Skip Quick Tools Links Quick Tools Track a Package Informed Delivery …

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element has children which are not allowed: li[tabindex]
`[role]`s are not contained by their required parent element (rule: aria-required-parent)

Some ARIA child roles must be contained by specific parent roles to properly perform their intended accessibility functions. Learn more about ARIA roles and required parent element.

Section 508 FPC: WV, WH, LM

Affected elements (10):

Element 1

Element path: nav > ul.nav-list > li.qt-nav > a.nav-first-element

Label: Quick Tools

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Required ARIA parents role not present: menu, menubar, group

Background and foreground colors do not have a sufficient contrast ratio. (rule: color-contrast)

Low-contrast text is difficult or impossible for many users to read. Learn how to provide sufficient color contrast.

Section 508 FPC: LV, WPC

Element path: div.col-12 > div.col-7 > div.d-md-none > a.inline-link

Heading elements are not in a sequentially-descending order (rule: heading-order)

Properly ordered headings that do not skip levels convey the semantic structure of the page, making it easier to navigate and understand when using assistive technologies. Learn more about heading order.

Section 508 FPC: WV, WH, LM

Image elements do not have `[alt]` attributes (rule: image-alt)

Informative elements should aim for short, descriptive alternate text. Decorative elements can be ignored with an empty alt attribute. Learn more about the `alt` attribute.

Section 508 FPC: WV, WH

Links do not have a discernible name (rule: link-name)

Link text (and alternate text for images, when used as links) that is discernible, unique, and focusable improves the navigation experience for screen reader users. Learn how to make links accessible.

https://urs.earthdata.nasa.gov

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 81

Axe findings: 5

Buttons do not have an accessible name (rule: button-name)

When a button doesn't have an accessible name, screen readers announce it as "button", making it unusable for users who rely on screen readers. Learn how to make buttons more accessible.

Section 508 FPC: WV, WH

Affected elements (1):

Element 1

Element path: div#tophat3Widget > nav#tophat3WidgetNavBar > div.container-fluid > button#tophat3HamburgerButton

  • Element does not have inner text that is visible to screen readers
  • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  • Element has no title attribute
  • Element does not have an implicit (wrapped) <label>
  • Element does not have an explicit <label>
  • Element's default semantics were not overridden with role="none" or role="presentation"

Some elements have a `[tabindex]` value greater than 0 (rule: tabindex)

A value greater than 0 implies an explicit navigation ordering. Although technically valid, this often creates frustrating experiences for users who rely on assistive technologies. Learn more about the `tabindex` attribute.

Section 508 FPC: WV, WH, LM

Lists do not contain only `<li>` elements and script supporting elements (`<script>` and `<template>`). (rule: list)

List items (`<li>`) are not contained within `<ul>`, `<ol>` or `<menu>` parent elements. (rule: listitem)

Screen readers require list items (`<li>`) to be contained within a parent `<ul>`, `<ol>` or `<menu>` to be announced properly. Learn more about proper list structure.

`[aria-*]` attributes do not match their roles (rule: aria-allowed-attr)

Each ARIA `role` supports a specific subset of `aria-*` attributes. Mismatching these invalidates the `aria-*` attributes. Learn how to match ARIA attributes to their roles.

Links rely on color to be distinguishable. (rule: link-in-text-block)

Screen readers have features to make navigating tables easier. Ensuring that tables use the actual caption element instead of cells with the `[colspan]` attribute may improve the experience for screen reader users. Learn more about captions.

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 96

Axe findings: 2

Some elements have a `[tabindex]` value greater than 0 (rule: tabindex)

A value greater than 0 implies an explicit navigation ordering. Although technically valid, this often creates frustrating experiences for users who rely on assistive technologies. Learn more about the `tabindex` attribute.

Links rely on color to be distinguishable. (rule: link-in-text-block)

Low-contrast text is difficult or impossible for many users to read. Link text that is discernible improves the experience for users with low vision. Learn how to make links distinguishable.

Section 508 FPC: LV, WPC

Affected elements (2):

Element 1

Element path: div#content > div.toolbar > div.search-helper > a

Snippet:

<a href="https://docs.ckan.org/en/2.10/user-guide.html?#search-in-detail" target="_blank">

Label: "Search in Detail" instructions.

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • The link has insufficient color contrast of 1.92:1 with the surrounding text. (Minimum contrast is 3:1, link text: #284a7a, surrounding text: #1b1b1b)
  • The link has no styling (such as underline) to distinguish it from the surrounding text

Element 2

Element path: div.dataset-content > div.notes > div > a

Snippet:

<a href="http://on.ny.gov/1GpWiHD" rel="noopener">

Label: http://on.ny.gov/1GpWiHD

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • The link has insufficient color contrast of 1.92:1 with the surrounding text. (Minimum contrast is 3:1, link text: #284a7a, surrounding text: #1b1b1b)
  • The link has no styling (such as underline) to distinguish it from the surrounding text
Links do not have a discernible name (rule: link-name)

Link text (and alternate text for images, when used as links) that is discernible, unique, and focusable improves the navigation experience for screen reader users. Learn how to make links accessible.

Section 508 FPC: WV, WH, LM

Affected elements (1):

Element 1

Element path: div.toolbar > ol.breadcrumb > li.home > a

Snippet:

<a href="/dataset/">

How to fix: Fix all of the following:

  • Element is in tab order and does not have accessible text
  • Fix any of the following:
  • Element does not have text that is visible to screen readers
  • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  • Element has no title attribute
Lists do not contain only `<li>` elements and script supporting elements (`<script>` and `<template>`). (rule: list)

Screen readers have a specific way of announcing lists. Ensuring proper list structure aids screen reader output. Learn more about proper list structure.

Section 508 FPC: WV, WH

Element path: div.primary > section.module > div.module-content > ul.dataset-list

List items (`<li>`) are not contained within `<ul>`, `<ol>` or `<menu>` parent elements. (rule: listitem)

Screen readers require list items (`<li>`) to be contained within a parent `<ul>`, `<ol>` or `<menu>` to be announced properly. Learn more about proper list structure.

Accessibility Findings

https://marine.weather.gov

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 67

Axe findings: 5

Background and foreground colors do not have a sufficient contrast ratio. (rule: color-contrast)

Low-contrast text is difficult or impossible for many users to read. Learn how to provide sufficient color contrast.

Section 508 FPC: LV, WPC

Affected elements (1):

Element 1

Element path: div.one-sixth-first > div#forecast-lookup > form#getForecast > input#inputstring

Snippet:

<input id="inputstring" name="inputstring" type="text" value="Enter location ..." onclick="this.value=''" autocomplete="off">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element has insufficient color contrast of 2.84 (foreground color: #999999, background color: #ffffff, font size: 8.0pt (10.6667px), font weight: normal). Expected contrast ratio of 4.5:1
`<html>` element does not have a `[lang]` attribute (rule: html-has-lang)

If a page doesn't specify a `lang` attribute, a screen reader assumes that the page is in the default language that the user chose when setting up the screen reader. If the page isn't actually in the default language, then the screen reader might not announce the page's text correctly. Learn more about the `lang` attribute.

Section 508 FPC: WV, WH, LLCLA

Affected elements (1):

Element 1

Element path: html

Snippet:

<html xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1999/xhtml">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • The <html> element does not have a lang attribute
Image elements do not have `[alt]` attributes (rule: image-alt)

Informative elements should aim for short, descriptive alternate text. Decorative elements can be ignored with an empty alt attribute. Learn more about the `alt` attribute.

Section 508 FPC: WV, WH

Heading elements are not in a sequentially-descending order (rule: heading-order)

Properly ordered headings that do not skip levels convey the semantic structure of the page, making it easier to navigate and understand when using assistive technologies. Learn more about heading order.

Section 508 FPC: WV, WH, LM

Affected elements (1):

Element 1

Element path: div.rich-text > div#choose-your-location-A > div.usa-space > h4.usa-accordion__heading

Snippet:

<h4 data-v-593890b4="" class="usa-accordion__heading">

Label: A

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Heading order invalid
`[user-scalable="no"]` is used in the `<meta name="viewport">` element or the `[maximum-scale]` attribute is less than 5. (rule: meta-viewport)

Disabling zooming is problematic for users with low vision who rely on screen magnification to properly see the contents of a web page. Learn more about the viewport meta tag.

Section 508 FPC: LV

Affected elements (1):

Element 1

Element path: head > meta

Snippet:

<meta name="viewport" content="user-scalable=no,initial-scale=1,maximum-scale=1,minimum-scale=1,width=dev…">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • user-scalable=no on <meta> tag disables zooming on mobile devices

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 90

Axe findings: 5

`[aria-hidden="true"]` elements contain focusable descendents (rule: aria-hidden-focus)

Focusable descendents within an `[aria-hidden="true"]` element prevent those interactive elements from being available to users of assistive technologies like screen readers. Learn how `aria-hidden` affects focusable elements.

Section 508 FPC: WV, LV, WH, LM

Affected elements (2):

Element 1

Element path: body.mat-typography > i94-root > mat-drawer-container.mat-drawer-container > div.cdk-visually-hidden

Snippet:

<div tabindex="0" class="cdk-visually-hidden cdk-focus-trap-anchor" aria-hidden="true">

How to fix: Fix all of the following:

  • Focusable content should have tabindex="-1" or be removed from the DOM

Element 2

Element path: body.mat-typography > i94-root > mat-drawer-container.mat-drawer-container > div.cdk-visually-hidden

Snippet:

<div tabindex="0" class="cdk-visually-hidden cdk-focus-trap-anchor" aria-hidden="true">

How to fix: Fix all of the following:

  • Focusable content should have tabindex="-1" or be removed from the DOM
Heading elements are not in a sequentially-descending order (rule: heading-order)

Properly ordered headings that do not skip levels convey the semantic structure of the page, making it easier to navigate and understand when using assistive technologies. Learn more about heading order.

Label: Visiting the U.S. & Arriving Via a Land Border or Selected Ferry?

Some elements have a `[tabindex]` value greater than 0 (rule: tabindex)

A value greater than 0 implies an explicit navigation ordering. Although technically valid, this often creates frustrating experiences for users who rely on assistive technologies. Learn more about the `tabindex` attribute.

Section 508 FPC: WV, WH, LM

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element has a tabindex greater than 0

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 97

Touch targets do not have sufficient size or spacing. (rule: target-size)

Touch targets with sufficient size and spacing help users who may have difficulty targeting small controls to activate the targets. Learn more about touch targets.

Section 508 FPC: LM, LRS
